summary: Iteration 7 — verified full CMS → landing chain for hero media URL changes. Cannot reproduce the user's complaint 'save does not change the video'. Every link of the chain works on the preview URL: (1) admin login OK, (2) MediaPicker input accepts pasted URL and its onChange fires and updates React state, (3) 'сохранить' button issues PUT /api/admin/content → 200, (4) toast 'Сохранено. Откройте сайт' appears, (5) GET /api/content immediately returns the new hero.video_url with Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate (no proxy override), (6) in a freshly opened landing tab, <video data-testid=hero-video><source src=…> reflects the new URL exactly (React re-mounts the <video> because of key={hero.video_url}), (7) same behaviour verified for hero.poster_url. After tests, both hero.video_url and hero.poster_url were RESTORED to their original defaults through the admin UI — final GET /api/content confirms video_url=mixkit default and poster_url=unsplash default.
action_items: User-reported bug ('изменения видео не применяются после Save') is NOT reproducible on the preview URL — the full save → refresh → DOM update chain works end-to-end. Most likely user-side causes to check: (a) browser HTTP cache / service worker on the user's browser cached the OLD /api/content response — user should hard-reload (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+R) or open in incognito; (b) user is looking at the same tab that already has the old <video> instantiated and the tab has not been reloaded (ContentContext.refresh WILL update it, but if the user is looking at a tab that was opened BEFORE the Admin tab and NEVER received the refresh signal, they won't see it — cross-tab sync is not implemented); (c) production domain may sit behind a CDN (Cloudflare) that caches /api/content even though origin sends no-store — verify with `curl -I https://detailing-autohaus.ru/api/content` on the production host and look for cf-cache-status: HIT.

OPTIONAL enhancement: add a cross-tab refresh via BroadcastChannel or window.postMessage so that if the user has the landing page open in another tab, saving in /admin automatically re-fetches /api/content there too. Currently ContentProvider only refetches on its own mount or when refreshLanding() is called from the SAME React tree (i.e. within /admin) — so the /admin tab's own preview updates, but a separately-opened /(landing) tab keeps the old content until manual reload.
critical_code_review_comments: ContentContext.refresh() correctly uses no-store because axios sends no explicit Cache-Control but the backend sets no-store — verified. If a CDN sits in front in production, add `?t=${Date.now()}` cache-buster to the GET /api/content call inside refresh() to bypass any misconfigured intermediate cache. This is a defensive one-liner.
Hero.jsx uses <video key={hero.video_url}> which correctly forces React to unmount+remount the <video> element when the URL changes — this is why the switch is instant. Good pattern.
MediaPicker.jsx onChange fires on every keystroke of the URL input (line 57). No debounce needed because state only lives locally until user clicks Save. Correct.

The 'открыть сайт' <a> in the admin header uses target='_blank' — a fresh tab means fresh ContentProvider mount, so new URL is always fetched. If user was clicking this link, they should see the change immediately. Confirmed in test.

Potential subtle bug: saveContent returns the merged content from backend and Admin.jsx setContent(saved). But if a user makes rapid edits + saves, they could lose the latest local edits because saved response arrives AFTER a subsequent onChange. Not blocking, but consider disabling inputs while saving OR merging saved response with any newer local state. Not in-scope for this bug report.

success_rate.backend: 100% (2/2 verified: PUT /admin/content and GET /api/content with no-store)

seed_data_creation: Test temporarily set hero.video_url=https://storage.yandexcloud.net/newautohaus/1.mp4 and hero.poster_url=https://storage.yandexcloud.net/newautohaus/1.mp4, then RESTORED to defaults (video_url=https://assets.mixkit.co/videos/35540/35540-720.mp4, poster_url=https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1503376780353-7e6692767b70?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1920&q=85). Verified via final GET /api/content.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL https://luxury-wrap.preview.emergentagent.com. Admin CMS save→landing update flow is fully functional (iter7 confirmed). If user complains that video/poster doesn't change, first check: (a) they are looking at a freshly-loaded / tab (not one opened before saving), (b) they don't have a stale service worker / cached response, (c) production doesn't have a CDN caching /api/content — the backend already sends 'cache-control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate' and it survives the preview's cloudflare proxy. Selector tip for MediaPicker: the label 'Фоновое видео' and 'Постер видео (картинка-заглушка)' partially overlap on the substring 'видео' — Playwright ':has-text' matches partially and picks the FIRST match (poster) which caused a misleading first-run failure. Use page.get_by_text(exact_label, exact=True) instead. MediaPicker inputs have no data-testid; they share placeholder='URL или /api/media/…'.
rca of the issue: USER REPORT COULD NOT BE REPRODUCED. Full E2E chain verified working end-to-end. Backend: PUT /api/admin/content persists hero.video_url; GET /api/content returns updated value with Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate (Cloudflare passes it through as DYNAMIC, not cached). Frontend: MediaPicker input onChange updates state; Admin.handleSave calls saveContent + refreshLanding + shows toast; ContentContext.refresh() re-fetches /api/content; Hero.jsx <video key={hero.video_url}> forces DOM remount when URL changes; new <source src> is applied. Screenshots + fetch responses confirm every step. Probable user-side causes (in order of likelihood): (1) browser cache — user needs hard reload; (2) user is viewing a landing tab that was opened before the save and never navigated/refreshed; (3) production CDN (if different from preview) caches /api/content — main agent should verify Cache-Control response on production domain and add a cache-buster query param to GET /api/content as a defensive measure.
